summary: The user sought information on upcoming conferences related to AI in healthcare, expressing particular interest in MLHC 2023 and the workshop on Explainable AI in Healthcare. The session covered notable conferences, key researchers, and specific presentations, including Dr. Ipsita Bhattacharya's work on explainable AI for clinical decision support systems.
facts: The user is interested in upcoming conferences on AI in healthcare.
MLHC 2023 will take place in New York City, NY, from June 1-3, 2023.
Dr. Ipsita Bhattacharya is a researcher at the University of California, San Francisco (UCSF).
Dr. Bhattacharya's research focuses on developing explainable AI models for clinical decision support systems.
The workshop on Explainable AI in Healthcare at MLHC 2023 will be organized by Dr. Marzyeh Ghassemi and Dr. Jayne Farris.
Dr. Bhattacharya will present on "Explainable AI for Clinical Decision Support Systems" at MLHC 2023.
The workshop will cover the importance of explainability in AI systems for healthcare.
Dr. Finale Doshi-Velez will discuss "Explainability in Healthcare: Why it Matters and How to Achieve it" at the workshop.
Dr. Been Kim will present on "Model Interpretability Techniques for Healthcare" at the workshop.
Dr. Bhattacharya's research includes developing AI-powered clinical decision support systems that provide personalized treatment recommendations.
The user expressed interest in learning more about machine learning applications in healthcare, particularly in clinical decision support systems.
The user requested recommendations for recent research papers or articles on machine learning in clinical decision support systems.
